title: France's political parties face uncertainty ahead of presidential race
subhead: With the local elections finished, the race for the presidency is kicking off. Except for the far right and the radical left, parties now face the pressing question of how to select their candidates for the 2027 presidential election.

War is transforming Ukraine's dogs who live on the front line
subhead: Canines found near combat zones are small, light and dark-colored, according to a study. These characteristics have helped them survive in a hostile environment.

title: Israeli parliament passes budget, allowing Netanyahu to avoid early elections
subhead: Opposition parties slammed the budget for increasing funding to Israel's ultra-Orthodox community, which refuses to allow its young men to serve in the military.

title: Spice up your life! Five restaurants in the Paris region where you can eat spicy food
subhead: Mexican chipotle, Korean kimchi, Tunisian harissa: Whether mild or fiery, these condiments add zest to dishes from around the world. Here are five addresses in Paris and Montreuil to awaken your taste buds.

title: France has yet to find a forward-looking political discourse on racism
subhead: While Bally Bagayoko, the newly-elected mayor of Saint-Denis, has responded with composure to the media storm unleashed against him, leftist leader Jean-Luc Mélenchon is 'feeding the identity-based rhetoric of the far right,' writes Le Monde columnist Philippe Bernard.

Flavel, a victim of police violence, tells his story: 'If there hadn't been videos, no one would ever have believed me'
subhead: On March 16, the former musician went out to order food in Noisiel, in the eastern Paris suburbs. Police violently arrested him, as seen in a video widely shared online. He described to Le Monde the events that led him to file a complaint with the internal affairs agency.

title: Christian Jambet, Islamic scholar: 'The true leaders of Iran are now soldiers who want to spread the Islamic Revolution beyond Iran's border'
subhead: The philosopher and member of the Académie Française, an expert on Shiite spiritualities, shares his thoughts on the ongoing war and the 'degeneration' of the Iranian regime.

title: The American dream crumbles for French students
subhead: Since Donald Trump's return to the White House, several institutions have reported a decline in applications to US campuses. Amid political tensions, stricter visa scrutiny and a shift favoring colleges in Asia, a subtle downturn is taking shape.
